_readme: Keys here MUST exactly match model_ids in models.json (case-sensitive,
including dots and dashes). backend.sh apply joins models.json + this file
by exact model_id and writes the api_key into .codex_env.local /
.claude_env.local at apply-time. The api_key never enters chat or agent
context — backend.sh apply is the only reader.
1. backend.sh init copies this file to models.secrets.json (chmod 600).
2. Replace placeholder REPLACE_WITH:sk-... values with your real keys.
3. Make sure each key here corresponds to a model_id in models.json that
Schema decision: keys are exact model_ids — NOT a composite string like
'secrets:gpt-5.5.api_key' (the dot in 'gpt-5.5' would collide with a dot
namespace separator). JSON keys preserve any character. This mirrors
Kubernetes secretKeyRef and LiteLLM's split between registry metadata and
Add ONE entry per model in models.json that needs an external api_key.
cursor-* models in models.json don't need entries here (Cursor handles auth).

_template._status: Generic placeholder. Rename to the exact model_id from models.json (e.g. 'gpt-5.5', 'claude-4.7-opus'). Entries starting with `_` are ignored.
